surface: Phased port (Phase 0 + A + Link shipped): the framework-agnostic router core (lib/router/* + framework-free helpers, ~12k lines) is vendored byte-close and validated by 161 of upstream's own router unit tests; the data-mode React layer (createMemoryRouter, RouterProvider incl. the /dom flushSync variant, Outlet, Await, RenderErrorBoundary/errorElement, Link + useLinkClickHandler, and the full read-hook family) is transcribed onto octane and differential-verified byte-identical against real react-router.
divergences: Refs are props (octane has no forwardRef) — Link's forwardRef becomes a `ref` prop.
Error-boundary reset on location change / revalidation-idle happens in a layout effect one commit after upstream's render-phase derivation — same observable outcome.

octane's flushSync inside an ambient flush degrades to a plain call drained at that flush's boundary (sync scroll/navigation notifies from within event handlers land at the flush boundary instead of nested) — consumer-invisible, conformance-pinned.
ssr: Not yet ported: StaticRouter/StaticRouterProvider/createStaticHandler/createStaticRouter (Phase F, on octane/server).
notes: Not ported yet (phased — docs/remix-router-port-plan.md): declarative <Routes>/<Route>/MemoryRouter/Navigate (Phase B), browser/hash routers + NavLink + useSearchParams (C), Form/useSubmit/useFetcher(s) (D), useBlocker/usePrompt/ScrollRestoration/useBeforeUnload/useViewTransitionState (E), static SSR + framework stubs + full export parity (F). Never: framework mode (needs @react-router/dev), RSC. tests/conformance/parity.test.ts pins the exact per-phase boundary.
